description_long: This is a large, bulbous terracotta vase with a wide, flared rim and a tall, narrow neck. The body of the vase is adorned with subtle, vertically oriented patterns that resemble stylized leaves or feathers, giving it a decorative yet earthy appearance. Two rounded handles are attached symmetrically on either side of the body, while a small, raised nub is present near the top of the main chamber. The surface has a matte, slightly rough finish, typical of hand-crafted pottery. The coloration is a warm, reddish-brown with some lighter, almost sandy gradients, suggesting natural clay firing. This vase would be well-suited for display in a public garden, park, or as a decorative element in an urban plaza.
description: A large, reddish-brown terracotta vase with a flared rim, tall neck, and decorative vertical patterns, featuring two side handles and a matte finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flared rim, tall neck, two side handles, and central decorative pattern with a small raised nub.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the curvature of the body, one handle, and the transition of color and pattern around the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back with both handles visible and minimal surface patterning.
description_view_3: Opposite angled view showing the other side handle, the continuation of the vertical pattern, and the vase's rounded form.
